blocks.body: By-phrase - indicates the doer of the action in the passive sentence. But "by" is not always needed: often the performer is obvious, unknown or unimportant. In this topic, you will learn how to use "by" correctly and avoid common mistakes.

{ "title": "6. Short summary", "items": [ "Add \"by\": when the performer is important, unexpected, or for contrast.", "Omit \"by\": when the performer is obvious, unknown, unimportant, or general (people, they).", "by = performer of the action (who/what caused it).", "with = tool, tool, material (made with).", "You can combine: \"painted by Monet with oil paints\".", "Avoid unnecessary \"by\" with obvious performers." ] }
